problems.text: Manual weekly reporting takes 3–4 hours every Monday
problems.quote: It takes about 3–4 hours every week. And honestly, by the time the report is done, some of the data is already outdated.

problems.text: Data inconsistency between tools causes wasted meeting time
problems.quote: We spend another hour in the meeting trying to reconcile the numbers instead of making decisions.
We're making decisions in Thursday leadership meetings based on Monday data.

motivations.text: Reduce manual analyst time so they can do higher-value work
motivations.quote: My best analyst is spending 4 hours every Monday doing work that a computer should do.

motivations.text: Share real-time data with the board without manual effort
motivations.quote: I want to be able to share it with my board without any manual work.

current_alternatives.name: Zapier
current_alternatives.why_inadequate: Kept breaking when tools updated their APIs

current_alternatives.name: BI Tool (unnamed)
current_alternatives.why_inadequate: Required a dedicated data engineer; not realistic for a team of 12

current_alternatives.name: Manual spreadsheet
current_alternatives.why_used: Current default — most reliable they've found
current_alternatives.why_inadequate: 3–4 hours per week, data is stale by the time it's ready

key_quotes.text: It takes about 3–4 hours every week. And honestly, by the time the report is done, some of the data is already outdated.
We're making decisions in Thursday leadership meetings based on Monday data. That's almost a week old.
key_quotes.context: Explaining the business impact of slow reporting

key_quotes.text: I just want one dashboard that updates automatically and shows me the 5–6 numbers I actually care about.
The last two tools we tried required 3-month implementation projects, and they both failed.
If you can solve that, I'll be your biggest advocate.
key_quotes.context: Closing statement about urgency of the problem

objections.text: IT security review required for any data integration
objections.quote: Any data integration needs to go through a security review.

objections.text: Skepticism about fast implementation promises based on past failures
objections.quote: I'm very skeptical of anything that promises it'll be up in a week.

objections.text: Cost scrutiny — board will challenge additional tool spend
objections.quote: Our board will ask why we're paying for another tool.

opportunities.text: Automated multi-source dashboard with 5–6 configurable KPIs
opportunities.evidence: Explicitly described as the ideal solution. Named 5 specific metrics.

opportunities.text: Board-ready reporting export — no manual formatting
opportunities.evidence: Maya specifically mentioned sharing with the board as a key requirement.

segment_signals.signal: IT security review required before adoption
segment_signals.implication: Enterprise segment — needs SOC 2, SSO, and IT approval flow

segment_signals.signal: Team of 12 — no dedicated data engineer
segment_signals.implication: Must be self-service; complexity is a disqualifier

recommended_follow_ups: Ask for a list of the 5–6 metrics Maya tracks to understand exact data model
Understand the IT security review process and timeline
Explore whether she has budget authority or needs board approval

evidence_link: Source: interview with Maya (Head of Operations, Streamline Corp) on 2025-01-10 via Grain
